item.title: MMC Port Is Said to Approach Bidders for Potential Stake Sale
item.description: Malaysian tycoon Syed Mokhtar Al-Bukhary’s MMC Port Holdings Sdn. has approached prospective bidders for a minority stake in the company, according to people familiar with the matter, after postponing an initial public offering last year.

item.title: Apollo Set to Hand Over Control of Reno de Medici to Bondholders
item.description: Apollo Global Management is set to hand over the keys of Reno de Medici SpA to its bondholders, as part of a debt restructuring, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Swiss Inflation Unexpectedly Holds Before SNB Rate Decision
item.description: Switzerland’s inflation rate came in lower in than economists expected last month, a sign the strength of the franc may be offsetting the impact of high energy costs.

item.title: Malaysian Palm Oil Exports to Face Pressure From Indonesian Push
item.description: Malaysian palm oil exports could tumble for a third straight month in June if buyers favor cheaper Indonesian supplies as Jakarta’s overhaul of commodity shipments sparks a push to move cargoes before the new rules fully take hold.

item.title: African Financier AFC Raises $2 Billion Fueled by Asian Banks
item.description: The Africa Finance Corp. raised $2 billion via a syndicated loan, with more than half provided by Asian and European banks seeking to capitalize on growing demand for infrastructure projects across the continent.

item.title: Sweden’s Subdued Inflation Gives Riksbank Rate Hike Reprieve
item.description: Sweden’s core inflation rate increased at a slower pace than the central bank had expected in May, giving it room to hold off on borrowing-cost increases in the wake of the Iran war.

item.title: Osaka Bourse to Add More Single-Stock Options for Market Makers
item.description: Osaka Exchange Inc. will increase the number of single-stock options in its market-making program in another effort to boost derivative use in Japan, bourse officials said.

item.title: New Zealand Bond Auction Draws Record Demand After Issuance Cuts
item.description: Demand for New Zealand’s 2031 government bonds surged to a record at auction after the government unexpectedly cut its projected borrowing needs for the coming years.

item.title: Indian Owner of Swiss Gold Refiner Probed for $159 billion of Inflated Revenue
item.description: India’s markets regulator has alleged that the owner of Swiss gold refiner Valcambi SA overstated revenue, according to an interim order issued Wednesday.

item.title: Russia Spring Wheat Planting Continues to Lag on Poor Weather
item.description: Russian spring wheat planting continues to face delays as persistent rain hinders growers, risking lower harvests.

item.title: FTSE 100 Falls as Oil Shares Weigh
RBA Is Already Seeing Signs Rate Hikes Are Working, Bullock Says
item.description: Australia’s central bank has already seen some signs that its interest-rate increases are starting to work through the economy, Governor Michele Bullock said, reiterating that the monetary policy board will do what’s necessary to achieve its mandate.

item.title: Asia Steps Up Currency Defense as Korea, Indonesia Vow Action
item.description: Asian authorities are ramping up their currency defense as high energy costs and bets that the Federal Reserve will raise interest rates pressure them.

item.title: Oil Rally Spurs Nigeria to Weigh Refinancing High-Cost Debt
item.description: Nigeria plans to refinance high-cost debt and raise funds to help plug its budget deficit, leveraging strong investor confidence amid elevated oil prices fueled by the US-Iran conflict.

item.title: India Money Market-Volumes Jump to Record as Bank Lending Booms
item.description: India’s money-market turnover jumped to a record as state-owned lenders stepped up borrowing to fund booming credit demand.

item.title: Peso Slump Prompts Philippines to Warn Against Forex Speculation
item.description: The Philippine central bank has cautioned banks against using foreign-exchange derivatives transactions as a tool to profit from currency volatility that has pushed the peso to a record low.

item.title: Kim Jong Un Tours New Nuclear Site, Says Output Capacity Doubled
item.description: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un has unveiled what state media described as a new nuclear materials production facility as he pushes to expand the country’s nuclear arsenal, amid reports China’s president may travel to Pyongyang in coming weeks.

item.title: Traders Brace for Big Yen Swings on BOJ, Intervention Risks
item.description: Options traders are ramping up hedges against sharp yen swings over the next two weeks, positioning for the Bank of Japan’s policy meeting and the possibility of currency intervention by authorities seeking to support the yen.

item.title: Korea’s Wealth Fund to Tap Alternative Assets With Tokyo Office
item.description: Korea Investment Corp. will open its first office in Tokyo this summer as the $232 billion sovereign wealth fund seeks to expand investments in Japan, including alternative assets such as private equity, hedge funds and private debt.

item.title: Cerebras Says It’s Working With All AI Gear Makers Except Nvidia
item.description: Cerebras Systems Inc. plans to cooperate with a wide variety of suppliers of AI data center components, opening the way for further partnerships that mirror its agreement with Amazon.com Inc.

item.title: BOJ Is Said to Mull June Rate Hike With Another Possible in 2026
item.description: Bank of Japan officials are set to consider a quarter percentage point increase to the benchmark interest rate this month and see the possibility of a further rate hike later this year, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: What If Vanguard's Va-Va-VOOm Follows Magellan?
item.description: The first trillion-dollar ETF brings excitement, but looming IPOs are unsettling passive funds.

item.title: Base Metals Dip on Middle East Uncertainty, Tech Stock Pullback
item.description: Copper and aluminum extended their retreat as US President Donald Trump continued his fraught push to reach a peace deal with Iran, while a pullback in technology stocks also weighed on sentiment.

item.title: Putin Turns Russia’s AI Future Into a Kremlin Family Business
item.description: President Vladimir Putin’s push for what he calls Russia’s technological sovereignty is turning into a family affair with the development of key sectors entrusted to one of his daughters and the children of close allies.

item.title: Netflix Aims to Use AI to Help Viewers Manage Content Overload
item.description: Netflix Inc. is using AI to help customers cut through the noise of content overload, said Elizabeth Stone, the streaming service’s chief product and technology officer.

item.title: Bank Indonesia Gets New Growth Mandate and Parliament Oversight
item.description: Indonesia has expanded the central bank’s objectives to include real sector growth, and granted parliament the power to evaluate the monetary authority’s performance.

item.title: Sunshine Silver Raises $270 Million in US IPO to Restart Mine
item.description: Sunshine Silver Mining & Refining Co. raised $270 million in a US initial public offering that priced at the low end of the marketed range, with the funds expected to be used for restarting production at an Idaho mine.

item.title: TSMC CEO Warns Chip Supply Won’t Meet AI-Fueled Demand for Years
item.description: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. Chief Executive Officer C.C. Wei said the company’s global chip supply will fall short of AI-fueled demand for years to come, sustaining revenue growth for the firm.

item.title: Iron Ore Falls as Fundamentals Soften, Fueling Bearish Mood
item.description: Iron ore fell to a two-month low, extending this week’s losses as rising supply and soft seasonal steel demand weighed on sentiment.

item.title: Indian IT Stocks Slump as AI Jitters Resurface
item.description: Investors remain far from convinced that the sector has found a bottom.
